critic_raw:
All evaluation systems (pendulum, body-spring, two/three-body) are conservative — they conserve energy by construction of the Hamiltonian framework. The methodology does not test systems with dissipation or external forcing, which violate the Hamiltonian assumption. This means the evaluation only tests the model on data where its inductive bias is exactly correct, providing no evidence of behavior when the physics are misspecified.

Forward and backward rollout accuracy are theoretically symmetric in exact Hamiltonian systems due to time-reversibility. However, learned discretization errors accumulate differently in each direction. Conflating forward and backward rollout under a single metric obscures whether the model learned a valid time-symmetric energy surface or simply memorized forward dynamics.

Pixel-level reconstruction metrics (MSE, SSIM) do not measure whether the learned Hamiltonian is physically meaningful. A model can achieve low reconstruction error by hallucinating visually plausible trajectories that violate energy conservation. No energy conservation diagnostic is reported.

The generative claim — sampling new trajectories — requires a well-calibrated posterior over initial conditions. If the variational posterior collapses (a common failure mode in VAE-based models), the model samples near-identical trajectories regardless of the conditioning, producing deterministic output disguised as generative diversity.
all_issues_raised: All evaluation systems are conservative by construction, ensuring the Hamiltonian inductive bias is always correctly specified; no dissipative or forced systems are tested, so misspecification behavior is entirely unknown.
Forward and backward rollout are evaluated under a single metric despite having asymmetric error accumulation in learned discretizations; conflation hides whether time-symmetry is actually learned.
Pixel-level reconstruction metrics cannot verify physical validity — a model can minimize MSE while violating energy conservation, and no energy conservation diagnostic is reported.

The generative trajectory sampling claim requires a well-calibrated posterior; variational posterior collapse would produce deterministic outputs that pass visual inspection but are not genuinely generative.
all_issues_adjudicated: All evaluation systems are conservative by construction, ensuring the Hamiltonian inductive bias is always correctly specified; no dissipative or forced systems are tested, so misspecification behavior is entirely unknown.
Forward and backward rollout are evaluated under a single metric despite having asymmetric error accumulation in learned discretizations; conflation hides whether time-symmetry is actually learned.
Pixel-level reconstruction metrics cannot verify physical validity — a model can minimize MSE while violating energy conservation, and no energy conservation diagnostic is reported.

The generative trajectory sampling claim requires a well-calibrated posterior; variational posterior collapse would produce deterministic outputs that pass visual inspection but are not genuinely generative.
